name: Lab Interpretation — Massive Liver Panel Elevation
description: Level 2 — MIMIC hadm 22205327 — massively elevated AST/ALT (>8000), acute hepatic injury pattern recognition
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 50-year-old female admitted with saddle pulmonary embolism, now developing acute hepatic failure:

Liver Function Tests:
- AST (Aspartate Aminotransferase): 8,035 IU/L (reference: 0–40 IU/L)
- ALT (Alanine Aminotransferase): 8,500 IU/L (reference: 0–40 IU/L)
- Total Bilirubin: 4.2 mg/dL (reference: 0–1.5 mg/dL)
- Albumin: 2.8 g/dL (reference: 3.5–5.2 g/dL)

Coagulation:
- INR: 3.0 (reference: 0.9–1.1)
- PT: 33.4 sec (reference: 9.4–12.5 sec)
- PTT: 65.4 sec (reference: 25–36.5 sec)
- Fibrinogen: 46 mg/dL (reference: 180–400 mg/dL)

Cardiac:
- Troponin T: 0.24 ng/mL (reference: 0–0.01 ng/mL)

Electrolytes:
- Bicarbonate: 15 mEq/L (reference: 22–32 mEq/L)
- Anion Gap: 23 mEq/L (reference: 8–20 mEq/L)

Provide clinical interpretation of these results.

abnormal_values.significance: Massive hepatocellular injury (200x upper limit) — ischemic hepatitis or massive hepatic necrosis
Massive hepatocellular injury — AST:ALT ratio near 1:1 suggests ischemic (not alcoholic) hepatitis
Severe coagulopathy reflecting hepatic synthetic dysfunction
Prolonged PTT contributing to coagulopathy risk
Critically low fibrinogen indicating consumptive coagulopathy (possible DIC) and/or hepatic failure
Cardiac injury from right heart strain due to massive pulmonary embolism
High anion gap metabolic acidosis — likely lactic acidosis from shock

expected.interpretation: Critical multi-system failure: massive ischemic hepatitis (AST/ALT >8000x from hepatic hypoperfusion in shock), severe coagulopathy with consumptive coagulopathy/DIC (INR 3.0, fibrinogen 46), right heart strain (troponin 0.24), and high-anion-gap metabolic acidosis from shock-related hypoperfusion.
expected.pattern_recognition: Ischemic hepatitis pattern: abrupt massive transaminase elevation (>1000x ULN, near equal AST:ALT) triggered by hemodynamic collapse from saddle PE. Compounded by: DIC (low fibrinogen + prolonged PT/PTT/INR) + lactic acidosis (high AG, low bicarb) + right heart injury (troponin).
differential_considerations: Ischemic hepatitis / shock liver (primary cause of transaminase elevation)
Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) from massive PE + hepatic failure
Massive pulmonary embolism with right heart failure (troponin elevation)
High anion gap metabolic acidosis from lactic acidosis in shock
